questions.question: National Project for Strengthening Disaster Risk Reduction (NPSDRR) is jointly implemented by which institutions?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) and National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)

options.label: B
options.text: NITI Aayog and Ministry of Home Affairs

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Home Affairs and Ministry of Panchayati Raj

options.label: D
options.text: National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) and NITI Aayog

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) and National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)

questions.explanation: A High-Level Committee chaired by Union Home & Cooperation Minister Amit Shah approved ₹507.37 crore for National Project for Strengthening Disaster Risk Reduction (NPSDRR) in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across 20 states. It is jointly implemented by Ministry of Panchayati Raj and National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA). It is linked to the National Disaster Mitigation Fund (NDMF) launched in 2021; the initiative is now extended to Panchayat level. The focus is to integrate Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) into governance using a bottom-up approach and build disaster-resilient communities.

questions.question: Where was National Conference for Chairpersons of Public Service Commissions inaugurated?
questions.explanation: President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated the National Conference for Chairpersons of Public Service Commissions in Hyderabad, Telangana on 19 December 2025. Public Service Commissions must uphold ideals of equality, justice and welfare as guided by Preamble, Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles. Commissions should ensure equality of opportunity and aim for equality of outcomes. They act as change agents promoting equity and support permanent executive continuity. Civil servants must be sensitive to women, marginalised and vulnerable groups; gender sensitisation required. Strong, effective governance is needed as India targets becoming third-largest economy and achieving Viksit Bharat by 2047.

questions.question: “Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs 2.0 (TIDE 2.0)” scheme is an initiative of which ministry?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

questions.explanation: Government follows a lifecycle-based approach to support startups from idea to product development and testing. Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs 2.0 (TIDE 2.0) is a program by Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. It was launched in 2019 to promote technology-based entrepreneurship in the Information and Communications Technology (ICT) sector. The scheme has a financial outlay of ₹264.62 crore for a five-year period and aims to support approximately 2,000 technology startups. As of now, 1,706 startups have been supported under TIDE 2.0, demonstrating strong progress in idea validation, prototype development, and product-market fit.

questions.question: International Human Solidarity Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: International Human Solidarity Day is observed annually on 20 December. The day highlights the importance of solidarity among people in addressing global challenges. It emphasizes collective efforts to eradicate poverty, hunger, and disease worldwide. The day also reminds governments to honour their commitments to international agreements and global development goals. Observing the day encourages cooperation, inclusiveness, and shared responsibility for a sustainable and equitable world.

questions.question: Which organization launched RESPOND Basket 2025?

options.label: C
options.text: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research

options.label: D
options.text: Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre

questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) recently launched RESPOND Basket 2025. The programme invites research proposals from premier universities and recognised academic and R&D institutions. It focuses on areas relevant to ISRO’s upcoming missions and national priorities. RESPOND Basket 2025 supports projects including the Bharatiya Antariksh Station, Chandrayaan-4, Gaganyaan missions, Venus orbiter, and a human Moon landing. The initiative aims to strengthen collaboration between ISRO and academic institutions and foster research aligned with India’s space ambitions.

questions.question: Which state has emerged as the top-performing state in India for claim settlements under Ayushman Bharat Scheme?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has emerged as the top-performing state in India for claim settlements under Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana–Mukhyamantri Amrutum (AB PMJAY–MA). The scheme integrates the Centre’s Ayushman Bharat programme with the state-run Mukhyamantri Amrutum Yojana, covering around 1.20 crore families in Gujarat. In July 2023, the annual health insurance cover per family was increased from Rs 5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh, enhancing financial protection against major illnesses. The scheme ensures timely, cashless, and inclusive access to medical treatment for citizens across income groups, including remote and economically weaker areas.

questions.question: Hormuz Island, that was recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Iran’s Hormuz Island recently gained global attention as its coastlines turned blood-red overnight, creating a surreal natural spectacle. Hormuz Island is a hilly island located in the Strait of Hormuz, between the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman, about 8 km off the coast of Iran. The island covers an area of 41.9 sq. km and is famous for its unique scenery and colorful soil, earning it the nickname “Rainbow Island”. Its terrain is composed of sedimentary rock and volcanic ash, and due to low precipitation, both the soil and water are salty.

questions.question: What is the name of the recent joint military operation launched by the United States and Jordan in Syria?
questions.explanation: United States (US) and Jordan launched Operation Hawkeye Strike in Syria after an ISIS attack in Palmyra killed two US soldiers and injured others. The operation involved overnight airstrikes on more than 70 ISIS targets using precision-guided weapons. The objective was to weaken ISIS fighters, weapons stockpiles, and infrastructure, disrupting future attacks. It aimed to send a strong deterrent message to terror groups and reaffirm the US policy of security through strength. The joint mission also highlighted US–Jordan military cooperation and commitment to regional counter-terrorism efforts.

questions.question: The National Maritime Heritage Complex (NMHC) is a flagship project of which ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

questions.explanation: India and the Netherlands signed an Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to cooperate on developing the National Maritime Heritage Complex (NMHC) at Lothal, Gujarat. National Maritime Heritage Complex (NMHC) is a flagship project of the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW) to showcase India’s 4,500–5,000-year-old maritime legacy. It is located in Lothal, near Ahmedabad, Gujarat. It will be India’s first comprehensive national institution dedicated exclusively to maritime heritage.

questions.question: Udanti Sitanadi Tiger Reserve is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Forest authorities in Chhattisgarh recently arrested 53 people for attempting to encroach on protected forest land within the Udanti–Sitanadi Tiger Reserve. The Udanti–Sitanadi Tiger Reserve is located in Chhattisgarh and forms a key wildlife conservation zone in central India. The reserve was established by merging two existing protected areas — the Sitanadi and Udanti Wildlife Sanctuaries. The reserve’s drainage network is dominated by the Mahanadi River, which acts as the main river system.
